questions.q: On what date was Shivaram Rajguru executed?
questions.explanation: Rajguru was hanged on 23 March 1931 in Lahore.

questions.q: Rajguru was executed alongside which two other revolutionaries?
questions.explanation: Rajguru was hanged with Bhagat Singh and Sukhdev on 23 March 1931.

questions.q: Rajguru was a member of which revolutionary organization?
questions.explanation: Rajguru belonged to the Hindustan Socialist Republican Association (HSRA).

questions.q: In which city was Rajguru executed?
questions.explanation: Rajguru was executed in Lahore Central Jail in 1931.

questions.q: From which present-day Indian state did Rajguru come?
questions.explanation: Rajguru was born in the village of Khed in Maharashtra, later renamed Rajgurunagar.

questions.q: For what skill was Rajguru especially known within the revolutionary group?
questions.explanation: Rajguru was reputed as an excellent marksman, earning his place as a shooter in HSRA actions.

questions.q: In which 1928 Lahore action did Rajguru take part with his comrades?
options: The killing of police officer John Saunders
questions.explanation: Rajguru took part in the shooting of Assistant Superintendent John Saunders in Lahore in December 1928.

questions.q: The Saunders killing was carried out to avenge the death of which leader?
questions.explanation: The HSRA killed Saunders to avenge Lala Lajpat Rai, fatally injured in a Simon Commission protest.

questions.q: Which fellow HSRA leader was closely linked to Rajguru in the Saunders action?
questions.explanation: Rajguru acted alongside Bhagat Singh in the shooting of Saunders in Lahore.

questions.q: In which year did the Saunders killing take place?
questions.explanation: The Saunders killing was carried out on 17 December 1928 in Lahore.

questions.q: After his name, the town of his birth in Maharashtra was renamed what?
questions.explanation: Rajguru's birthplace Khed was renamed Rajgurunagar in his honour.

questions.q: What was Rajguru's full name?
questions.explanation: His full name was Shivaram Hari Rajguru.

questions.q: In which conspiracy case was Rajguru tried and sentenced to death along with Bhagat Singh?
questions.explanation: Rajguru was tried and condemned in the Lahore Conspiracy Case for the Saunders killing.

questions.q: Which British officer was the actual target the revolutionaries intended to kill in Lahore in 1928?
questions.explanation: The intended target was Superintendent James Scott, who had ordered the lathi charge on Lajpat Rai; Saunders was killed in a case of mistaken identity.

questions.q: Rajguru was arrested in 1929 in which city while travelling?
questions.explanation: Rajguru was arrested at Pune (Poona) in 1929 and later handed over for the Lahore Conspiracy Case.

questions.q: Roughly how old was Rajguru at the time of his execution in 1931?
questions.explanation: Rajguru, born in 1908, was about 22 years old when he was hanged in 1931.

questions.q: In which year was Rajguru born?
questions.explanation: Rajguru was born on 24 August 1908.

questions.q: Rajguru reportedly studied scriptures and Sanskrit in which holy city in his youth?
questions.explanation: Rajguru went to Varanasi (Banaras) as a youth, where he studied and came into contact with revolutionary circles.

questions.q: Which slogan, shared by his organization, did Rajguru and his comrades raise in defiance of British rule?
questions.explanation: Rajguru and his HSRA comrades raised the slogan 'Inquilab Zindabad', meaning 'Long Live the Revolution'.

questions.q: The 1928 lathi charge that the Saunders killing avenged was during a protest against which commission?
questions.explanation: Lala Lajpat Rai was injured protesting the Simon Commission; his death was avenged by the Saunders killing.

questions.q: What was the name of the larger revolutionary network whose Punjab actions Rajguru joined?
questions.explanation: Rajguru was part of the Hindustan Socialist Republican Association, which carried out the Lahore actions.

questions.q: Through which contact did Rajguru first become associated with revolutionary activity in Banaras?
options: Members of the Indian National Congress
Revolutionaries linked to the HSRA network
Officers of the Indian National Army
questions.explanation: In Banaras, Rajguru came into contact with revolutionaries connected to the HSRA network and was drawn into the movement.

questions.q: In the Saunders shooting, what role is Rajguru generally credited with?
options: Firing the first shots at Saunders
questions.explanation: Rajguru, an able marksman, is generally credited with firing the initial shots at Saunders, with Bhagat Singh firing further rounds.

questions.q: After the Saunders killing, Rajguru fled Lahore and was eventually traced to which region?
questions.explanation: Rajguru returned toward Maharashtra and was arrested in the Pune area before being taken for trial in Lahore.

questions.q: The three Lahore martyrs were cremated secretly by British authorities near the bank of which river?
questions.explanation: The bodies of Rajguru, Bhagat Singh and Sukhdev were taken to the banks of the Sutlej near Hussainiwala for secret cremation.

questions.q: At which site near Ferozepur is a memorial to Rajguru and his comrades located?
questions.explanation: The National Martyrs Memorial at Hussainiwala near Ferozepur honours Rajguru, Bhagat Singh and Sukhdev.

questions.q: Rajguru's family belonged to which traditional priestly community, reflected in the surname 'Rajguru'?
options: A Brahmin family of royal priests
questions.explanation: The surname Rajguru reflects his Brahmin family's traditional role as priests to a princely or royal house.

questions.q: In which year was Rajguru arrested before facing trial in the Lahore Conspiracy Case?
questions.explanation: Rajguru was arrested in 1929, the same year the wider Lahore Conspiracy Case unfolded.

questions.q: On what date in December 1928 did Rajguru take part in the Saunders shooting?
questions.explanation: The Saunders shooting took place on 17 December 1928 in Lahore.

questions.q: Rajguru's execution alongside Bhagat Singh and Sukhdev triggered protests during which ongoing Gandhian campaign?
questions.explanation: The 1931 executions occurred during the Civil Disobedience era and stirred widespread anger across the country.
